title: 3D simultaneous seismic refraction and reflection tomography of wide-angle data from the Central Chilean Margin
abstract: Three-dimensional (3D) simultaneous seismic refraction and reflection tomography is applied to wide-angle data from the central Chilean margin, collected in 1995 by GEOMAR in a region where the Juan Fernández Ridge on the Nazca Plate is subducting beneath South America. The 3D velocity model reveals the large-scale margin structure and establishes the relationship between seamount subduction, plate deformation and seismicity patterns.

location.description: Central Chilean margin at ~32°S where the Juan Fernández Ridge subducts beneath South America; 3D tomography volume

data.name: CINCA95 OBH 3D Wide-Angle Array — Central Chile ~32°S
data.description: 3D wide-angle OBH data from CINCA95 (SO104) at ~32°S used for first 3D simultaneous refraction-reflection tomography of the Chilean margin; reveals Juan Fernández Ridge structure and seamount subduction. [CINCA95 cruise (SO104, 1995) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: CINCA95 MCS Profiles — Central Chile ~32°S
data.description: Coincident MCS reflection data used as constraints in the 3D tomographic inversion. [CINCA95 cruise (SO104, 1995) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.notes: Multibeam bathymetry from SO104 used to define seafloor geometry for tomographic modeling. [CINCA95 cruise (SO104, 1995) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Multibeam bathymetry from SONNE cruises SO104 and SO244 — north Chile

_data_note: Uses same CINCA95 (SO104) OBH dataset as Flueh et al. 1998. First 3D tomographic inversion for this margin.

processing.summary: Wide-angle OBH data from the 1995 CINCA95 cruise (SO104) were used in 3D simultaneous refraction and reflection tomographic inversion to derive a 3D P-wave velocity model of the central Chilean margin at ~32°S. The 3D model captures the along-strike structural variability of the Juan Fernández Ridge collision zone and the relationship between seamount subduction and seismicity.
workflow: 1. OBH data sorting and instrument relocation
processing.notes: First 3D tomographic inversion for the Chilean margin; short GRL letter

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. 4 authors confirmed. Hojka first name expanded to 'André M.' from PDF title page. vessel updated to R/V SONNE (SO104, CINCA95/CONDOR, 1995). Data: 10 OBH deployed (7 recovered, 3 instrument failures), 10 airgun profiles, 90×90 km 3D survey area at ~32°S. First 3D simultaneous refraction-reflection tomography of Chilean margin. Companion to Flueh et al. 1998.
